texts.full_text: 70 sqm close to a school and park: Look at other apartments around the same size in areas nearby schools/parks. 4. Rentability Calculation Estimate potential rental income based on comparable rents from your research, factoring local market trends. 5. Cost of Ownership - Calculate expenses such as property taxes (if applicable), insurance costs and maintenance costs to get a clear picture of the profitability after deductions are made. 6. Adjustments for Uniqueness Make necessary changes if there’s something unusual about your rental that sets it apart from others, either positively or negatively affecting its value. 7. Professional Help - Consider hiring an experienced appraiser to ensure accuracy and professionalism in the valuation process which can be useful even when dealing with high-value properties. 8. Stay Updated on Market Trends Continuously monitor changes that may affect property values, such as new developments or changes in local economy. By following these steps diligently, you can determine a fair rental value
